# examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml
#
# Run 2 of 3 in the TIDE-on-TIDE dogfood sequence.
# Apply ONLY after 01-analytics-project reaches `status.phase: Complete`.
# Do NOT apply 03-project-editor-project.yaml until this Project reaches
# `status.phase: Complete` — the Project CRD has no ordering mechanism;
# sequencing is operator discipline.
#
# Purpose: add a second Subagent implementation (Codex / OpenAI) to TIDE and
# demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — actually running mixed-provider
# waves, not just proving the interface compiles.
#
# After this run completes: the run's token spend, dispatch behavior, and
# failure characteristics are visible on the analytics dashboard built by run 1.
# Run 3 (Project editor) can then be authored and applied through the dashboard
# surface run 3 itself will build.
#
# Prerequisites (see README.md for full recipe):
#   - 01-analytics-project must have reached status.phase: Complete
#   - kind cluster + TIDE charts installed (see docs/INSTALL.md)
#   - tide-secrets Secret created in namespace tide-dogfood-codex
#     (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + GIT_PAT — same as analytics namespace)
#   - openai-secrets Secret created in namespace tide-dogfood-codex
#     (OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY — see README.md)
#   - per-namespace-resources applied per README.md
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: tide-dogfood-codex
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
    tideproject.k8s/sample: dogfood-codex
---
apiVersion: tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1
kind: Project
metadata:
  name: dogfood-codex-runtime
  namespace: tide-dogfood-codex
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
    tideproject.k8s/sample: dogfood-codex
spec:
  # TIDE operates against its own repo — TIDE-on-TIDE dogfood run.
  targetRepo: https://github.com/jsquirrelz/tide.git

  outcomePrompt: |
    ## Outcome

    Add a second Subagent implementation for the Codex runtime (OpenAI) and
    demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — mixed-provider waves that actually
    run, not just interface scaffolding. Concrete outcome: a TIDE operator can
    configure a Project where the planner pool runs Claude and the executor pool
    runs Codex, and TIDE dispatches a multi-phase run to completion with correct
    wave-boundary failure semantics across both providers.

    ## Architecture — locked decisions (do NOT reinvent these)

    **Implementation location: internal/subagent/codex/**
    Mirror the existing internal/subagent/anthropic/ layering exactly:
    - internal/subagent/codex/client.go — Client struct + constructor
    - internal/subagent/codex/run.go — Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
    - internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile — multi-stage build mirroring anthropic/Dockerfile
    - internal/subagent/codex/doc.go — package doc referencing D-C1 layering pattern

    All OpenAI/Codex-specific code stays behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent
    interface. No provider hard-coding in the controller. No Codex imports
    in internal/controller/ or cmd/manager/.

    **Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project).**
    Planner pool and executor pool each pin a runtime. This is the correct
    granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves real
    heterogeneity within a single run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity
    of per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen):
    - Per-task selection: large API surface, non-trivial scheduler changes.
    - Per-project selection: no mixing within a run (defeats heterogeneity goal).

    The SubagentConfig field already has Levels.{Milestone,Phase,Plan,Task}
    per-level LevelConfig overrides (api/v1alpha1/project_types.go). Wire the
    Codex image at the levels the operator wants to run Codex.

    **Credential path.**
    OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY via a K8s Secret — the established
    Secret-ref pattern from providerSecretRef. Never mount host config, never
    headless OAuth (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100 — same restriction applies
    to Codex). Chart values for the new Secret name are part of the deliverable.

    **Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics.**
    The spec's failure handling (spec section "Failure handling at wave
    boundaries") applies unchanged regardless of which provider runs a task:
    - Failed task: siblings in the same wave continue (they were declared
      independent).
    - Dependents in later waves: never dispatch.
    - Non-dependents in later waves: dispatch in strict profile.
    This contract must not change when tasks run on different providers.

    **Codex CLI facts — carry verbatim, do NOT re-research:**
    - codex exec is first-class headless: one-shot, no TTY, final message
      to stdout.
    - Flags for clean containers: --ephemeral, --skip-git-repo-check,
      --ignore-user-config.
    - --json emits a JSONL event stream.
    - --output-schema <file> constrains the final response to a JSON schema
      — use this for child-CRD emission (child-CRD format is JSON, mandated).
    - Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner needs --sandbox workspace-write
      (fine inside an already-isolated pod — mirrors the Claude runner posture).
    - API-key env auth (OPENAI_API_KEY) is the documented container path —
      no headless-OAuth problem.
    - MCP client support (STDIO + streamable HTTP) via config.toml or
      codex mcp add — available if needed for child-CRD emission.
    - Sources: developers.openai.com/codex/{noninteractive,cli/reference,
      auth,cli/features} (verified 2026-06).

    **Container image.**
    internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile must build clean. The final image
    bundles the Codex CLI binary. Image reference convention mirrors
    ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0 (replace "claude" with
    "codex" in the image name; exact tag/registry are authoring decisions).

    ## Constraints

    - All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in internal/subagent/codex/ and
      behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent interface — no provider leakage into
      the controller or cmd/manager.
    - Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves.
    - Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — reference
      by Secret name only.
    - Follow all CLAUDE.md conventions: Apache 2.0 headers, logr/zap logging,
      controller-runtime patterns.
    - Do NOT break existing tests. Confirm make test is green before
      authoring any plan as delivered.
    - Do NOT add a real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit test-env
      guard (OPENAI_API_KEY env set) — tests must pass offline.

  # Phase 2 D-C1..C4: credproxy reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from this Secret.
  # For the planner pool (Claude side of the heterogeneous run).
  # The Codex executor pool's OPENAI_API_KEY lives in a separate Secret —
  # see README.md for the openai-secrets creation step.
  providerSecretRef: tide-secrets

  # Starting cap for dogfood runs — tune before applying.
  # These are multi-phase real work runs; the cap is a safety net.
  budget:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000
    rollingWindowCapCents: 10000
    rollingWindowDuration: 24h

  # Default image + model for the planner pool (Claude).
  # Codex executor pool is wired via subagent.levels.task.image override
  # once the codex subagent image is built by this run.
  # Cheaper alternatives: model: claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5
  subagent:
    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0
    model: claude-opus-4-7

  # Phase 3 D-B6: per-project git target + creds.
  git:
    repoURL: https://github.com/jsquirrelz/tide.git
    credsSecretRef: tide-secrets

  # Human oversight at milestone and phase level; plan and task auto.
  # Fully-autonomous: change milestone and phase to auto.
  gates:
    milestone: approve
    phase: approve
    plan: auto
    task: auto
    pauseBetweenWaves: false

  planAdmission:
    fileTouchMode: strict

  maxAttemptsPerTask: 3

{"apiVersion":"tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1","kind":"TaskEnvelopeIn","taskUID":"2d06aa90-d40b-4fdc-8412-d90191ddf3a3","role":"planner","level":"phase","prompt":"## Outcome

Add a second Subagent implementation for the Codex runtime (OpenAI) and
demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — mixed-provider waves that actually
run, not just interface scaffolding. Concrete outcome: a TIDE operator can
configure a Project where the planner pool runs Claude and the executor pool
runs Codex, and TIDE dispatches a multi-phase run to completion with correct
wave-boundary failure semantics across both providers.

## Current foundation — ALREADY IN MAIN, build ON it, do NOT rebuild

Much of what early drafts of this run assumed it would build now ships in
main (milestone v1.0.2 "Spring Tide" + late v1.0.1). Treat all of this as
given infrastructure; the ONLY new product surface this run delivers is the
Codex subagent package + the per-level provider switch.

- **v1alpha2 schema + global Execution DAG (Phases 23-26).** `Plan`/`Phase`/
  `Milestone`/`Task` all carry `dependsOn` at any level; the assembler builds
  ONE global Execution DAG and derives Project-scoped waves
  (`tide-wave-<project>-<N>`) across plan/phase/milestone boundaries via
  layered Kahn. The project planner emits N Milestones up-front with
  `Milestone.dependsOn` (planning-DAG edge only — zero execution edges).
  Child CRDs authored by the planner are v1alpha2.
- **Global dispatch / failure / gates / resumption (Phase 25).** Readiness is
  re-derived from the global indegree map + completed-set each reconcile
  (nothing cached). `failureProfile` and the gate-as-hold model below are live.
- **Cost / budget / telemetry / cache (Phases 16, 18-21).** `pkg/dispatch.Usage`,
  `tide_cost_cents_total`, budget enforcement, and the dashboard Telemetry tab
  + cache-efficiency panel all exist. The locked metric label set is
  {project,phase,plan,wave}; the `task` label is FORBIDDEN (cardinality analyzer).
- **Dashboard (Phase 26).** A global execution-DAG view renders the whole-Project
  wave DAG (dagre LR); run #2's waves/spend are watchable live.

## Architecture — locked decisions (do NOT reinvent these)

**Implementation location: internal/subagent/codex/**
Mirror the existing internal/subagent/anthropic/ layering exactly:
- internal/subagent/codex/client.go — Client struct + constructor
- internal/subagent/codex/run.go — Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
- internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile — multi-stage build mirroring anthropic/Dockerfile
- internal/subagent/codex/doc.go — package doc referencing D-C1 layering pattern

All OpenAI/Codex-specific code stays behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent
interface. No provider hard-coding in the controller. No Codex imports
in internal/controller/ or cmd/manager/.

**Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project).**
Planner pool and executor pool each pin a runtime. This is the correct
granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves real
heterogeneity within a single run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity
of per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen):
- Per-task selection: large API surface, non-trivial scheduler changes.
- Per-project selection: no mixing within a run (defeats heterogeneity goal).

The SubagentConfig field already has Levels.{Milestone,Phase,Plan,Task}
per-level LevelConfig overrides (api/v1alpha2/project_types.go via
SubagentConfig). Wire the Codex image at the levels the operator wants Codex.
NOTE: today `resolveImage` honors a per-level image, but `ResolveProvider`
(internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go) still returns Vendor:"anthropic"
unconditionally — so a non-Anthropic image at a level is selected but then
rejected at runtime (the image's vendor sentinel expects an anthropic
envelope). Adding the per-level vendor/provider switch IS part of this run's
deliverable.

**Credential path.**
OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY via a K8s Secret — the established
Secret-ref pattern from providerSecretRef. Never mount host config, never
headless OAuth (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100 — same restriction applies
to Codex). Chart values for the new Secret name are part of the deliverable.

**Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics.**
The spec's failure handling (spec section "Failure handling at wave
boundaries") applies unchanged regardless of which provider runs a task:
- Failed task: siblings in the same wave continue (they were declared
  independent).
- Dependents in later waves: never dispatch.
- Non-dependents in later waves: dispatch in strict profile.
This contract must not change when tasks run on different providers. It is now
enforced by the global indegree model (Phase 25); do not add a parallel path.

**Usage accounting + cost — REQUIRED for budget + observability.**
The cost/budget/telemetry surfaces (Phases 16, 18-21) depend on every Subagent
populating pkg/dispatch.Usage. The Codex Run() MUST normalize the OpenAI/Codex
usage block into that struct, or budget enforcement, tide_cost_cents_total, AND
the cache-efficiency dashboard panel will all read $0 / blank for Codex:
- InputTokens        <- usage.prompt_tokens
- OutputTokens       <- usage.completion_tokens
- CacheReadTokens    <- usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens
  (OpenAI caching is AUTOMATIC, marker-free, process-independent, 1,024-token
  floor — there is NO cache_control to set and NO separate "cache creation"
  concept, so CacheCreationTokens stays 0. This differs from Anthropic, whose
  field is cache_read_input_tokens / cache_creation_input_tokens.)
- EstimatedCostCents + CacheSavingsCents <- computed by a Codex-side pricing
  table mirroring internal/subagent/anthropic/pricing.go (per-model cents/MTok;
  cacheRead is OpenAI's discounted cached-input rate). Without this table the
  run cannot honor its own budget cap and the dashboard cannot attribute Codex
  spend. Keep the locked metric label set {project,phase,plan,wave} — the
  `task` label remains FORBIDDEN (metriccardinality analyzer).
The provider-neutral SharedContext field (Phase 20) on EnvelopeIn is shared
Go struct state — the Codex subagent receives it for free; no special handling.

**Codex CLI facts — carry verbatim, do NOT re-research:**
- codex exec is first-class headless: one-shot, no TTY, final message
  to stdout.
- Flags for clean containers: --ephemeral, --skip-git-repo-check,
  --ignore-user-config.
- --json emits a JSONL event stream.
- --output-schema <file> constrains the final response to a JSON schema
  — use this for child-CRD emission (child-CRD format is JSON, mandated).
- Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner needs --sandbox workspace-write
  (fine inside an already-isolated pod — mirrors the Claude runner posture).
- API-key env auth (OPENAI_API_KEY) is the documented container path —
  no headless-OAuth problem.
- MCP client support (STDIO + streamable HTTP) via config.toml or
  codex mcp add — available if needed for child-CRD emission.
- Sources: developers.openai.com/codex/{noninteractive,cli/reference,
  auth,cli/features} (verified 2026-06).

**Container image.**
internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile must build clean. The final image
bundles the Codex CLI binary. Image reference convention mirrors
ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent (replace "claude" with
"codex" in the image name; exact tag/registry are authoring decisions).

## Constraints

- All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in internal/subagent/codex/ and
  behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent interface — no provider leakage into
  the controller or cmd/manager.
- Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves.
- Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — reference
  by Secret name only.
- Follow all CLAUDE.md conventions: Apache 2.0 headers, logr/zap logging,
  controller-runtime patterns; keep make verify-* guards green.
- Do NOT break existing tests. Confirm make test is green before
  authoring any plan as delivered.
- Do NOT add a real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit test-env
  guard (OPENAI_API_KEY env set) — tests must pass offline.

file.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{ include "tide.fullname" . }}-controller-manager
  labels:
    control-plane: controller-manager
  {{- include "tide.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  replicas: {{ .Values.controllerManager.replicas }}
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
      control-plane: controller-manager
    {{- include "tide.selectorLabels" . | nindent 6 }}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
        control-plane: controller-manager
      {{- include "tide.selectorLabels" . | nindent 8 }}
      annotations:
        kubectl.kubernetes.io/default-container: manager
        {{- with .Values.controllerManager.manager.podAnnotations }}
        {{- toYaml . | nindent 8 }}
        {{- end }}
    spec:
      containers:
      - args:
          {{- toYaml .Values.controllerManager.manager.args | nindent 10 }}
          - --credproxy-image={{ .Values.images.credProxy.repository }}:{{ .Values.images.credProxy.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}
          - --default-file-touch-mode={{ .Values.planAdmission.fileTouchMode | default "warn" }}
          - --rate-limit-default-rpm={{ .Values.rateLimits.defaults.requestsPerMinute | default 60 }}
          - --rate-limit-default-burst={{ .Values.rateLimits.defaults.burst | default 10 }}
          - --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents={{ .Values.budget.reservePerDispatchCents | default 100 }}
          {{- if .Values.pricing.overrides }}
          - --pricing-overrides-json={{ .Values.pricing.overrides | toJson }}
          {{- end }}
          # phase2-args-injected
        command:
        - /manager
        env:
        - name: WATCH_NAMESPACE
          value: {{ quote .Values.controllerManager.manager.env.watchNamespace }}
        - name: KUBERNETES_CLUSTER_DOMAIN
          value: {{ quote .Values.kubernetesClusterDomain }}
        - name: TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE
          value: "{{ .Values.images.tidePush.repository }}:{{ .Values.images.tidePush.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}"
        # Phase 13 D-01: subagent image default channel.
        # Resolution chain (highest priority wins):
        #   Levels.<level>.Image (Project CRD per-level override)
        #   → Spec.Subagent.Image (Project CRD per-project default)
        #   → CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE (this env — Helm chart default)
        #
        # The startup flag injecting the stub image has been removed (Phase 13
        # D-01 deliberate fixed-contract exception). This env is now the sole
        # default tier.
        #
        # Stub opt-in for tests/CI:
        #   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:<tag>
        # Tag- or digest-qualified values (containing ":" after last "/" or "@sha256:")
        # pass through verbatim. Bare refs get ":<appVersion>" appended automatically.
        # Digest pinning passes through unchanged — recommended for production.
        - name: CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE
          {{- $img := required "subagent.defaults.image must be a non-empty image ref (e.g. ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:latest)" .Values.subagent.defaults.image }}
          {{- if or (regexMatch ":[^/]+$" $img) (contains "@" $img) }}
          value: {{ $img | quote }}
          {{- else }}
          value: {{ printf "%s:%s" $img .Chart.AppVersion | quote }}
          {{- end }}
        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_MILESTONE
          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.milestone.model | default "claude-opus-4-7" }}"
        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PHASE
          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.phase.model | default "claude-sonnet-4-6" }}"
        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PLAN
          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.plan.model | default "claude-sonnet-4-6" }}"
        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TASK
          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.task.model | default "claude-haiku-4-5" }}"
        - name: TIDE_LEADER_LEASE_SECONDS
          value: "{{ .Values.leaderElection.leaseDurationSeconds | default 15 }}"
        - name: TIDE_LEADER_RENEW_SECONDS
          value: "{{ .Values.leaderElection.renewDeadlineSeconds | default 10 }}"
        - name: TIDE_LEADER_RETRY_SECONDS
          value: "{{ .Values.leaderElection.retryPeriodSeconds | default 2 }}"
        # phase3-env-injected
        # Phase 4 plan 04-14 (D-O3): OTel env vars read by internal/otelinit.
        # Empty OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT → no-op TracerProvider (zero
        # overhead, default posture for plain clusters). Sampler is env-driven
        # to honor Pitfall 24 mitigation (no WithSampler in code).
        - name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.exporter.endpoint }}
        - name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER
          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.tracesSampler }}
        - name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG
          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.tracesSamplerArg }}
        - name: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.serviceName }}
        # phase4-env-injected
        - name: TIDE_REPORTER_IMAGE
          value: "{{ .Values.images.tideReporter.repository }}:{{ .Values.images.tideReporter.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}"
        # phase9-reporter-env-injected

        envFrom:
        - secretRef:
            name: {{ .Values.signingKey.secretName | default "tide-signing-key" }}
        image: {{ .Values.controllerManager.manager.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.controllerManager.manager.image.tag
          | default .Chart.AppVersion }}
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /healthz
            port: 8081
          initialDelaySeconds: 15
          periodSeconds: 20
        name: manager
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
          name: metrics
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 8081
          name: health
          protocol: TCP

Now I have a complete picture. The dispatch job pod spec already handles `ProviderSecretRef` by mounting it as an `envFrom: secretRef`. This means the OPENAI_API_KEY just needs to be in the same secret (or a different secret that's also referenced). 

Looking at the current mechanism:
- `Project.Spec.ProviderSecretRef` points to ONE secret
- That secret gets mounted as `envFrom: secretRef` on the dispatch pod
- So both ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and OPENAI_API_KEY could be in the same secret, OR we need a second SecretRef

For Codex, the approach per MILESTONE.md is:
> `OPENAI_API_KEY` (or `CODEX_API_KEY`) is delivered via a same-namespace K8s Secret using the established `providerSecretRef` pattern.

This suggests adding a second field like `codexProviderSecretRef` to the Project spec, though the existing `ProviderSecretRef` is already a single field that gets mounted, so I could either extend it or add a new one.

The simpler path would be to let operators put both keys in the same secret, but the MILESTONE.md language about "following the providerSecretRef pattern" points toward a dedicated field. Adding `codexProviderSecretRef` to `ProjectSpec` would mirror the existing pattern, though that requires an API type change and code regeneration. Finalizing the phase brief and plan breakdown for the heterogeneous dispatch demo — the Helm chart needs the Codex subagent image configuration and per-level vendor defaults, the API gains the `CodexProviderSecretRef` field for OpenAI credential injection, and the manager needs logic to resolve vendor settings at each level. Regenerating the deepcopy file, adding a second secret mount for the Codex provider reference, updating the environment resolution logic to handle per-level vendors, and creating both a heterogeneous project example and integration test to verify the mixed-provider setup works end-to-end with proper verification gates.

Now I'm laying out the implementation plan in waves, starting with wiring up the Helm chart surface for the Codex provider — configuring the image default, environment variables, and vendor-level defaults. ideproject.k8s_projects.yaml` — then for Wave 2, I'm adding a heterogeneous demo project that mixes OpenAI and Codex providers across different task levels, along with an integration test that validates mixed-provider workflows and confirms that failed Codex tasks allow sibling tasks to proceed while blocking dependents.

I need to guard all OpenAI API calls with environment variable checks or short test flags, then ensure the full test suite passes. Now I'm reviewing the spec more carefully to understand the exact task directory structure. Looking at the task structure, I need to create child Plan CRD JSON files in the children/ directory, and the harness will handle writing out.json. I should also write a markdown file for human review of the phase brief, probably as PHASE_BRIEF.md in the children/ directory.

Since the orchestrator only reads *.json files from children/, any markdown files there will be safely ignored by the automation but available for human review. Now I'm examining the Plan spec structure more carefully to understand how to define the phaseRef, dependencies, and other required fields.

I'm mapping out the specific plans with their wave assignments, dependencies, and the files each one touches—starting with the chart provider wiring and secret injection plans, then continuing through the remaining phases.

cat > /workspace/envelopes/2d06aa90-d40b-4fdc-8412-d90191ddf3a3/children/PHASE_BRIEF.md << 'EOF'
# Phase Brief — phase-05-heterogeneous-dispatch-demo

**Phase:** `phase-05-heterogeneous-dispatch-demo`
**Milestone:** `milestone-03-hetero-integration`
**Status:** Planned

---

## Objective

Deliver the operator-facing configuration surface and end-to-end integration
demonstration for mixed-provider (Claude + Codex) dispatch. Earlier phases ship
the Codex subagent package (phases 01–02), the Codex container image (phase 03),
and the per-level vendor switch in the controller (phase 04). This phase wires
the Helm chart to expose those capabilities, closes the OPENAI_API_KEY credential
path, and exercises the full mixed-provider dispatch loop with a reference Project
YAML and an offline-safe integration test.

The waveboundary failure contract — failed task siblings continue, dependents
never dispatch, non-dependents dispatch in strict profile — must hold identically
regardless of which provider ran a task; no new enforcement path is added here
(the global indegree model from Phase 25 is authoritative and unchanged).

---

## Scope

**In scope:**

- `charts/tide/values.yaml` — add `images.codexSubagent` block; add
  `subagent.defaults.codexImage`; add per-level vendor default keys
  (`subagent.levels.{milestone,phase,plan,task}.vendor`) whose values flow
  through as `TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR_*` env vars on the manager Deployment.
- `charts/tide/templates/deployment.yaml` — emit `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE` env var
  (resolution mirror of `CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE`); emit `TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR_*`
  env vars consuming the phase-04 `ResolveProvider` vendor precedence chain.
- `api/v1alpha1/project_types.go` — add `CodexProviderSecretRef` to `ProjectSpec`
  (schema-additive, backwards-compatible); regenerate CRD manifest + deepcopy.
- `internal/dispatch/podjob/jobspec.go` — mount `CodexProviderSecretRef` as a
  second `envFrom: secretRef` on dispatch Job pods, mirroring the existing
  `ProviderSecretRef` mount; injects `OPENAI_API_KEY` (or any key in the named
  Secret) without inlining credentials.
- `cmd/manager/env.go` + `cmd/manager/env_test.go` — add
  `resolvePerLevelVendors()` reading `TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR_{MILESTONE,PHASE,PLAN,
  TASK}`; extend `ProviderDefaults` (or `tideHelmProviderDefaults`) to carry
  the per-level vendor map alongside the existing per-level model map so
  `ResolveProvider` (phase-04) has a Helm-default vendor to fall through to.
- `examples/projects/hetero/project.yaml` — reference operator Project YAML
  with planner pool pinned to Claude and executor pool pinned to Codex via
  `subagent.levels.task.vendor: openai` + `subagent.levels.task.image:
  ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent` + `codexProviderSecretRef`.
- `examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml` — update to consume
  the new `codexProviderSecretRef` + per-level vendor fields.
- `test/integration/kind/hetero_dispatch_test.go` — mixed-provider integration
  test; all real OpenAI API calls are guarded by `OPENAI_API_KEY != ""` and
  `testing.Short()`; exercises D4 failure semantics (forced Codex task failure →
  sibling continues, dependents skipped, non-dependents dispatch); asserts
  `tide_cost_cents_total` + cache-efficiency panel carry non-zero Codex spend
  under `{project,phase,plan,wave}` label set (no `task` label).

**Out of scope (prior phases):**

- `internal/subagent/codex/` package implementation — phase 02
- `internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile` + `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go` — phase 03
- `api/v1alpha1/project_types.go` `LevelConfig.Vendor` field + `ResolveProvider`
  vendor chain — phase 04
- CRD manifest + deepcopy for `LevelConfig.Vendor` — phase 04

---

## Expected Deliverables

| # | Artifact | Owned by |
|---|----------|----------|
| 1 | `charts/tide/values.yaml` — `images.codexSubagent`, vendor defaults | plan-01 |
| 2 | `charts/tide/templates/deployment.yaml` — `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE`, `TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR_*` envs | plan-01 |
| 3 | `cmd/manager/env.go` + `env_test.go` — `resolvePerLevelVendors()`, updated defaults | plan-01 |
| 4 | `api/v1alpha1/project_types.go` — `CodexProviderSecretRef` field | plan-02 |
| 5 | `api/v1alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go` — regenerated | plan-02 |
| 6 | `config/crd/bases/tideproject.k8s_projects.yaml` — regenerated | plan-02 |
| 7 | `internal/dispatch/podjob/jobspec.go` — second `envFrom: secretRef` for Codex | plan-02 |
| 8 | `internal/dispatch/podjob/jobspec_test.go` — new Secret-mount assertion | plan-02 |
| 9 | `examples/projects/hetero/project.yaml` — heterogeneous reference Project | plan-03 |
| 10 | `examples/projects/dogfood/02-codex-runtime-project.yaml` — updated | plan-03 |
| 11 | `test/integration/kind/hetero_dispatch_test.go` — mixed-provider integration test | plan-03 |

---

## Verification Gates

1. `make test` exits 0 — all existing unit and integration tests pass unmodified.
2. `make verify-import-firewall` + `make verify-dispatch-imports` green — no
   `internal/subagent/codex` or OpenAI SDK import leaks into
   `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/`.
3. `helm template charts/tide` renders `CODEX_SUBAGENT_IMAGE`, `TIDE_DEFAULT_VENDOR_*`
   env vars, and the `codexProviderSecretRef` Secret mount across enabled/disabled
   permutations without secret material inlined.
4. A Project with `spec.subagent.levels.task.vendor: openai` resolves
   `Vendor: "openai"` from `ResolveProvider` while its planner levels resolve
   `Vendor: "anthropic"` — confirmed by the phase-04 unit test suite (not
   new work here; gate is that phase-04 tests still pass).
5. The `hetero_dispatch_test.go` integration test: (a) skips cleanly when
   `OPENAI_API_KEY` is unset (offline-safe); (b) when run with credentials,
   dispatches a two-wave Project to completion across both providers; (c)
   reproduces D4 failure semantics for a forced Codex task failure.
6. `tide_cost_cents_total` and the cache-efficiency panel attribute non-zero
   Codex spend under `{project,phase,plan,wave}` — `task` label absent
   (metriccardinality analyzer green).
7. No secret material appears in any checked-in manifest or chart value —
   credentials are referenced by Secret name only.
8. `make manifests` + `make generate` exit 0 and produce no uncommitted diff
   after plan-02 lands.

---

## Plan DAG

```
Wave 1 (parallel)
  plan-01-chart-codex-provider-wiring  ─────────┐
  plan-02-codex-secret-injection       ─────────┤
                                                  ▼
Wave 2                                plan-03-hetero-demo-and-integration-test
```

`plan-01` and `plan-02` touch disjoint file sets and declare no dependency on
each other — they may execute in parallel within Wave 1. `plan-03` depends on
both: the example Project YAML uses the chart values added in plan-01, and the
integration test exercises the Secret injection added in plan-02.
EOF

Touches `api/v1alpha1/project_types.go`, `zz_generated.deepcopy.go`, the CRD manifest, `podjob/jobspec.go`, and `jobspec_test.go`. No chart or env.go changes. The `CodexProviderSecretRef` field is optional (`+optional`, `omitempty`), so it is backwards-compatible — existing Projects without the field are unaffected.

**Wave 2 — single join barrier, depends on both wave-1 plans:**

- **`plan-03-hetero-demo-and-integration-test`** — the reference example Project YAML consumes the chart values added in plan-01 (per-level vendor keys) and the `codexProviderSecretRef` field added in plan-02; the integration test exercises both. Placed in wave 2 to guarantee the example and test reflect the complete, post-plan-01/02 contract rather than a moving target.
